description: Implement the main orchestration engine in internal/engine/. This is the core loop that ties everything together: loads tasks, builds DAG, initializes lifecycles, runs worker goroutines that claim tasks, execute agents, validate, review, merge, and handle conflicts. Includes crash recovery (reset stale in-progress tasks to ready on startup) and DAG-reactive scheduling (re-evaluate readiness after every merge). Use sync.Cond or channels to wake workers when new tasks become ready. Reference docs/implementation-plan.md Milestone 9 for the Engine struct, Run() algorithm, resume support, and retry vs crash recovery semantics.
commit: feat(engine): add orchestration engine with DAG-reactive scheduling and crash recovery
steps: Create internal/engine/engine.go with the Engine struct holding config, store, dag, worktree manager, agent factory, validator, reviewer factory, merger, resolver, maxParallel, and sync primitives
Implement New() constructor wiring all dependencies
Implement Run(ctx): load tasks, generate run ID (run-{YYYYMMDD}-{HHmmss}-{4hex}), build DAG, initialize lifecycles, run crash recovery, evaluate initial readiness, start worker goroutines

Implement the worker loop: ClaimNextReady -> create worktree -> running -> execute agent -> validating -> validate -> reviewing -> review -> merging -> merge -> merged (with retry and conflict resolution paths at each stage)
Implement crash recovery: on startup, reset stale running/validating/reviewing/merging/resolving tasks back to ready, clean up orphan worktrees
Implement DAG-reactive readiness: after every merge, re-evaluate all pending tasks and transition newly-ready ones, signal waiting workers via sync.Cond or channel
Implement retry logic: on failed_validation or rejected, check CanRetry, if yes transition to ready with ResumeFrom context and feedback from Error field; if no transition to failed
Implement conflict handling: on merge conflict, transition to conflict -> resolving, run resolver, retry merge; respect MaxResolveAttempts

Create internal/engine/engine_test.go: test 3 independent tasks run in parallel, test dependency chain A->B->C executes in order, test diamond dependency, test agent failure + retry, test validation failure + retry, test resume from partially-completed state
acceptance_criteria: Engine runs up to maxParallel workers concurrently
Workers claim tasks atomically via ClaimNextReady
Full happy path works: pending -> ready -> running -> validating -> reviewing -> merging -> merged
DAG dependencies are respected: dependent tasks only start after prerequisites merge
After each merge, pending tasks are re-evaluated for readiness
Crash recovery resets stale in-progress tasks to ready on startup
Retry preserves worktree and passes ResumeFrom + feedback to agent
Crash recovery destroys worktree and starts fresh
Conflict resolution respects MaxResolveAttempts before failing
Run returns a summary of results (merged/failed counts, timing)
